sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Schenker, dated July 28 and 29, 1931
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: The border tax has been lifted, so Oppel will be able to get to Galtür in August. – He has been reading Chrysander’s biography of Handel, which he finds musically weak.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker,Receipt of this letter is recorded in Schenker’s diary for July 31, 1931: “Von Oppel (Br.): kommt am 7. oder 8. VIII.” (“From Oppel (letter): he is coming on 7 or 8 August.”).

tei:div.tei:p: schönen Dank für die Karte vom 24.FWriting of this postcard is recorded in Schenker’s diary for July 24, 1931: “An Oppel (K.): vielleicht doch noch eine Wendung zur freien Einreise?!” (“To Oppel (postcard): perhaps there might still be a change, to permit free travel?!”). Die lästige Bestimmung ist ja nun aufgehoben, d.h. die Regierung hat ja nun wohl eingesehen, dass mit so blöden Massnahmen gar nichts erreicht wird. Ich fahre am 2. VIII abends hier ab u. werde 7. oder 8. August in Galtür sein.The Oppel visit is recorded in Schenker’s diary on August 7, 8, and 9: “4:45: Oppel takes afternoon snack in our room; then tuition and conversation until 7:10 – frightfully tiring, because it amounts to nothing; he wants to write about me as a "creator of language," to collect my coinage of phrases and terms, perhaps write about me in Der Kunstwart or in the Basler Zeitung. He compares me with Lessing! ‘You should not have to teach any more…’” “10:30 to 11:45 up the valley, Oppel meets us from the opposite direction: something on the fugue, then at the piano (only ¼ hour): he plays Corelli to me, I play Joseph Strauß’s Lob der Frauen to him. — 2:30 to 4: the Fugue in F minor finally solved and finished. – At 4 o’clock (Lie-Liechen is still asleep!) on the balcony, I warn Oppel again about Handel! Scarlatti crossed himself whenever his name came up; we must do so a thousand times – keep your brain away from his improvisations, even in the fugues. — Tea in our room, taken together!” — “At 4:45, Oppel meets us while we are playing solitaire; then we continue: Mazurka from Op. 30, according to the autograph manuscript. (supplementary note!): from 11:10 to 12:10, Oppel: the Fugue in F minor gone over in accordance with my recently completed model; notes on this in the lessonbook.” – Entries concerning Oppel continue each day August 10–17, with Oppel departing the last day. Hoffentlich geht es Ihren Augen besser. –

Die Londoner KonferenzThe London Conference was convened to address the German financial crisis, and met from July 17 to August 11. verlief, wie ich’s erwartet hatte. Die Franzosen geben nicht nach. – Inzwischen habe ich mich ländisch betätigt, Badezimmer, Flur etc. getüncht; es sehr nötig, nachdem seit 25 Jahren nichts in der Wohnung gemacht worden ist. Und so eine körperliche Ausspanung hat auch ihren Nutzen, die geistige Arbeit freut einen dann doppelt. –

29. VII. Am 31. kommt Ulrich zurück, und das ist mir sehr lieb, denn ich mag m. Frau u. den Kleinen nicht allein in der Wohnung lassen; jeden Augenblick kommen Bettler u. fremde Menschen, gegen die eine alleinstehende Frau meist hilflos ist. –

Ich las einen Roman von Anita Brück: Schicksale hinter Schreibmaschinen,Christa Anita Brück, Schicksale hinter Schreibmaschinen (Berlin: Sieben Stäbe-Verlag, 1930).
Typisch für unsre Zeit, u. genau so unreif wie Remarque’s „im Westen nichts Neues.“Erich Maria Remarque, Im Westen nichts Neues (Berlin: Propyläen Verlag, 1929), first published serially in the Vossische Zeitung (1928); published in English translation as All Quiet on the Western Front (London: G. P. Putnam, 1929). Einseitig, mit sozialistischer Tendenz, von Leutschen geschrieben, d.h. schlecht durchdacht, die genau eine Persönlichkeit vorstellen möchten, aber leider keine sind. –
Chrysander’s HändelFriedrich Chrysander, G. F. Händel, 3 vols (Leipzig: Breitkopf & Härtel, 1858–67). lese ich wieder, mir jetzt unerträglich, musikalisch gänzlich ungenügend. –
Ich gehe nun ein paar Tage mit unserm Secretär vom Kon. ins Kauner Tal, um von da aus ein paar Gletscher zu besteigen, dann kommt Galtür mit allen Freuden u. geistigen Hochgenüssen. –
Furtwängler dirigiert in Bayreuth Toscanini die Eroica vor!!

tei:closer.tei:salute: Alles Gute u. die herzlichsten Grüsse

tei:address.tei:addrLine: Leipzig July 28, 1931. W31. Könneritzstrasse 58III left side

tei:div.tei:p: Many thanks for your postcard of the 24th. The annoying regulation has now been lifted, i.e., the government has apparently realized that nothing is achieved with such stupid measures. I am leaving here on the evening of August 2 and will be in Galtür on August 7 or 8. Hopefully your eyes are better. –

The London conference went as I expected. The French are not giving in. – In the meantime, I have been busy with household chores, cleaning the bathroom, hallway, etc.; it was very necessary, as nothing had been done in the apartment for twenty-five years. And such physical relaxation also has its benefits; intellectual work is then twice as enjoyable. –

July 29: Ulrich is coming back on the 31st, and I am very happy about that, because I don't like to leave my wife and the little one alone in the apartment; beggars and strangers come by every moment, against whom a woman alone is usually helpless. –

I read a novel by Anita Brück: Fates Behind Typewriters:
typical of our time, and just as immature as Remarque's All Quiet on the Western Front; one-sided, with a socialist tendency, written by people who want to portray a personality but unfortunately are not one themselves. –
I am reading Chrysander’s biography of Handel again, which I now find unbearable, musically completely inadequate. –
I am now going to the Kammer Valley for a few days with our secretary from the Conservatory, to climb a few glaciers from there, then comes Galtür with all its joys and intellectual pleasures. –
Furtwängler is conducting in Bayreuth, Toscanini is performing the “Eroica”!

tei:closer.tei:salute: All the best and the most cordial greetings
to you and your dear wife
